MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JOHN COMPAGNO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Appearances: Randall S. Farrimond of the Farrimond Law Offices (415.362.4900) for defendant and moving party; Diego B. Flores of Shartsis Friese LLP (415.421.6500) appearing via Zoom for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued. The Court denies the defense's request to orally raise its written objections. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. There are multiple triable issues of fact, including but not limited to, 5, 6, 8, 13 and 14. Nazir v. United Airlines (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 243, 252. Additionally, there are triable issues of fact regarding equitable estoppel. Defendant's August 29, 2024, filings submitted without any citation to authority and leave of Court 8 days before the continued hearing are stricken. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. DEFENDANT JOHN COMPAGNO , AN INDIVIDUAL MOTION OF DEFENDANT JOHN COMPAGNO TO QUASH NOTICE OF DEPOSITION OF CRISTINA BARRING (PART 2 OF 2 FOR PURPOSES OF ENTRY OF TENTATIVE RULING)
Off Calendar Aug-02-2024 Off Calendar

Motion Of Defendant John Compagno To Quash Subpoena
Off Calendar Aug-05-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DIEGO B. FLORES, Esq. of Shartsis Friese LLP (Tel: 415-421-6500) for plaintiff Elias Chahwan;
Atty. RANDALL S. FARRIMOND, Esq. of Farrimond Law Offices (Tel: 415-362-4900) for defendant John Compagno.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JOHN COMPAGNO's Motion to Quash Subpoena (re: Holland & Knight LLP).

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. Counsel for each side authorizes the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling except as to the issue of sanctions which the Court takes under submission.

Defendant Compagno moves to quash a subpoena addressed to Holland & Knight LLP and related notice on grounds the same are allegedly untimely and fail to satisfy the notice requirements of CCP section 1985.3. The file sought by the subpoena pertains to legal representation of Plaintiff Chahwan by Defendant Compagno as a partner in Holland & Knight. Compagno claims that the file contains personal records pertaining to him, that he is a consumer because he transacted business with Holland & Knight, and that he did not receive a consumer records notice pursuant to CCP section 1985.3(e).

Defendant's motion requires the assumptions that a lawyer can be a consumer vis a vis his former client, that Compagno, a law partner, "transact[ed] business" with his law firm, that Chahwan's client file contains personal records of Compagno as responsible attorney, that any rights in Compagno's personal records were not waived to the extent they are part of Chahwan's client file, and that the file in question does not already belong to Plaintiff Chahwan under Rule of Professional Conduct 1.16(e)(1) and related authorities. These assumptions lack evidentiary or legal support. The Motion to Quash must be denied.

The Court takes the matter of sanctions under submission.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (525/JPT)

DEFENDANT JOHN COMPAGNO , AN INDIVIDUAL MOTION OF DEFENDANT JOHN COMPAGNO TO QUASH SUBPOENA (PART 2 OF 2 FOR PURPOSES OF ENTRY OF TENTATIVE RULING)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Aug-02-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DIEGO B. FLORES, Esq. of Shartsis Friese LLP (Tel: 415-421-6500) for plaintiff Elias Chahwan;
Atty. RANDALL S. FARRIMOND, Esq. of Farrimond Law Offices (Tel: 415-362-4900) for defendant John Compagno.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ELIAS CHAHWAN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. Counsel for each side authorizes the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications, as follows:

Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to requests for production of documents is granted as to Request No. 6, provided that the Request is hereby limited, as proposed by Plaintiff, to non-privileged communications referring or relating to Chahwan as an individual, and to exclude communications to which Chahwan was a party. Counsel for parties are ordered to meet and confer further to attempt to simply the instructions and definitions, and to reach an agreement on an appropriate deadline by which defendant shall serve further responses and produce relevant documents. The plaintiff may request a further hearing on the matter before Judge Pro Tem James Fleming if the parties are not able to reach an agreement.

Plaintiff's counsel is instructed to prepare a proposed order in Word Perfect format and submit it to the pro tem judge at jameshfleming@gmail.com (cc'ing the opposing party).

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (525/JPT)

Motion Of Defendant John Compagno To Quash Notice Of Deposition Of Cristina Barring
Off Calendar Aug-02-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DIEGO B. FLORES, Esq. of Shartsis Friese LLP (Tel: 415-421-6500) for plaintiff Elias Chahwan;
Atty. RANDALL S. FARRIMOND, Esq. of Farrimond Law Offices (Tel: 415-362-4900) for defendant John Compagno.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JOHN COMPAGNO's Motion To Quash Notice Of Deposition Of Cristina Barring.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. Counsel for each side authorizes the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Compagno moves to stay the deposition of Cristina Barring and to quash the Notice of Deposition Subpoena for Personal Appearance of Cristina Barring on the grounds that the Notice was untimely and fails to satisfy the notice requirements set forth in CCP sec. 2025.270. Plaintiff Chahwan contends that the deposition subpoena was served on a date allowing a reasonable time for the witness to travel to the place of the deposition under CCP sec. 2020.220(a) such that no deposition notice was necessary to compel the witness to travel to attend.

Plaintiff's argument misconceives the statutory scheme for taking depositions and compelling the attendance of witnesses and further seeks to create a conflict between Sections 2020.220 and 2025.270 when none exists.

Service of a deposition notice is the foundational step in taking a deposition. CCP secs. 2025.210 and 2025.220(a). An oral deposition must be scheduled for a date at least 10 days after service of the deposition notice, subject to a motion or ex parte application to shorten or extend time. CCP sec. 2025.270. If a subpoena is required to compel appearance of the witness, it must be served in adequate time for the witness to travel to the place of deposition. CCP sec. 2020.220(a). Plaintiff cites no authority that compliance with Section 2020.220 trumps the requirements of section 2025.270. The opposite is true; both sections must be obeyed.

Notice of litigation proceedings is an obvious requirement of due process of law. Plaintiff's attempt to avoid the notice provisions of Section 2020.270 must fail. The motion to quash notice of the Barring deposition is granted and the deposition of Barring is stayed, subject to renotice in compliance with CCP sec. 2025.270.

The Court finds that Plaintiff's opposition to the motion to quash was without substantial justification. Accordingly, Plaintiff Chahwan and his attorneys are ordered to pay sanctions of $1500 Defendant Compagno on or before August 9, 2024.

Defense counsel is instructed to prepare a proposed order in Word Perfect format and submit it to the pro tem judge at jameshfleming@gmail.com (cc'ing the opposing party).

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (525/JPT)
